title: At the Sports Club
summary: A new member joins a sports club and speaks with the coach about training schedules, equipment, and her fitness goals.

body.speaker: Member
body.text: Hi, I just joined the club last week. I'm not sure where to start.

body.speaker: Coach
body.text: Welcome! My name's David. I'm the head coach here. What's your name?

body.speaker: Member
body.text: I'm Lisa. I've never really trained at a sports club before, so I'm a bit nervous.

body.speaker: Coach
body.text: Don't worry, everyone feels that way at the beginning. What sport are you interested in?

body.speaker: Member
body.text: I'd like to try swimming and maybe some gym work. I want to get fitter.

body.speaker: Coach
body.text: That's a great combination. The pool is open from six in the morning until nine at night.

body.speaker: Member
body.text: That's good. Do I need to book a lane, or can I just turn up?

body.speaker: Coach
body.text: For the pool, you can just turn up on weekdays. At weekends, it's better to book in advance because it gets quite busy.

body.speaker: Member
body.text: OK, I'll keep that in mind. What about the gym? Is there an induction session?

body.speaker: Coach
body.text: Yes, all new members must complete an induction before using the gym equipment. I can book you in for one this Thursday at five o'clock.

body.speaker: Member
body.text: Thursday at five works perfectly for me. Do I need to bring anything?

body.speaker: Coach
body.text: Just wear comfortable sports clothes and bring a water bottle. Trainers are essential — no bare feet in the gym.

body.speaker: Member
body.text: Of course. How long does the induction take?

body.speaker: Coach
body.text: About an hour. We'll go through all the machines and I'll help you set some personal goals.

body.speaker: Member
body.text: That sounds really helpful. Thank you so much, David.

body.speaker: Coach
body.text: My pleasure. See you on Thursday, and enjoy your first swim!

annotations.exampleEn: She is the head coach of the national swimming team.

annotations.meaningVi: hơi lo lắng / hơi hồi hộp
annotations.exampleEn: I'm a bit nervous about the exam tomorrow.
Running and yoga is a great combination for fitness.
You need to book a lane at the pool for Saturday morning.

annotations.meaningVi: xuất hiện / đến (mà không báo trước)
annotations.exampleEn: You don't need a reservation — just turn up.
I always book a table in advance at busy restaurants.
Prices may change — keep that in mind when you plan your budget.
New staff must attend an induction session on their first day.
Please wipe down the gym equipment after use.

annotations.meaningVi: giày thể thao là bắt buộc
annotations.exampleEn: Trainers are essential for any gym session.
The trainer helped me set personal goals for the next three months.

annotations.meaningVi: rất vui được giúp bạn (đáp lại lời cảm ơn)

grammarNotes.title: Modal verb 'must' for obligation: 'All new members must complete an induction'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Must + verb nguyên thể' diễn tả sự bắt buộc — điều gì đó là cần thiết và không thể bỏ qua. 'All new members must complete an induction before using the gym equipment.' Đây là quy định của câu lạc bộ. So sánh: 'must' (bắt buộc, từ quy tắc hoặc người nói) và 'have to' (bắt buộc từ hoàn cảnh bên ngoài). Trong ngữ cảnh chính thức như quy định của câu lạc bộ, 'must' rất phổ biến.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'must + bare infinitive' to express obligation — something is required and cannot be skipped. Example: 'All new members must complete an induction.' This is a club rule. 'Must' often expresses internal or institutional obligation. Compare: 'You must wear trainers' (rule) vs 'You have to wear trainers' (external requirement). Both are acceptable here, but 'must' is common in formal rules and signs.

grammarNotes.title: 'I'd like to' for polite requests and wishes
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I'd like to + verb nguyên thể' là cách lịch sự và tự nhiên để diễn tả mong muốn hoặc yêu cầu. 'I'd like to try swimming' — Tôi muốn thử bơi lội. Đây lịch sự hơn 'I want to try swimming.' Bạn cũng có thể dùng 'I'd like to book an induction' khi yêu cầu dịch vụ. Đây là một trong những cách diễn đạt quan trọng nhất trong giao tiếp hàng ngày.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'I'd like to + bare infinitive' for polite wishes or requests. It is more formal and polite than 'I want to.' Examples: 'I'd like to try swimming.' / 'I'd like to book a session.' In service interactions (shops, sports clubs, restaurants), 'I'd like to' is the standard polite form. You can also offer: 'Would you like to...?' for suggestions.

translationVi:
Thành viên: Xin chào, tôi vừa tham gia câu lạc bộ tuần trước. Tôi không biết bắt đầu từ đâu.
Huấn luyện viên: Chào mừng! Tôi tên David. Tôi là huấn luyện viên trưởng ở đây. Bạn tên gì?
Thành viên: Tôi là Lisa. Tôi chưa bao giờ tập luyện tại câu lạc bộ thể thao trước đây, nên tôi hơi lo lắng.
Huấn luyện viên: Đừng lo, ai cũng cảm thấy vậy lúc đầu. Bạn quan tâm đến môn thể thao nào?
Thành viên: Tôi muốn thử bơi lội và có thể tập gym. Tôi muốn rèn luyện sức khỏe.
Huấn luyện viên: Đó là sự kết hợp tuyệt vời. Hồ bơi mở cửa từ sáu giờ sáng đến chín giờ tối.
Thành viên: Tốt quá. Tôi có cần đặt trước một làn bơi không, hay có thể đến trực tiếp?
Huấn luyện viên: Đối với hồ bơi, bạn có thể đến thẳng vào các ngày trong tuần. Vào cuối tuần thì nên đặt trước vì khá đông.
Thành viên: OK, tôi sẽ ghi nhớ điều đó. Còn phòng gym thì sao? Có buổi hướng dẫn nhập môn không?
Huấn luyện viên: Có, tất cả thành viên mới phải hoàn thành buổi hướng dẫn trước khi sử dụng thiết bị phòng gym. Tôi có thể đặt lịch cho bạn vào thứ Năm này lúc năm giờ.
Thành viên: Thứ Năm lúc năm giờ hoàn toàn phù hợp với tôi. Tôi có cần mang theo gì không?
Huấn luyện viên: Chỉ cần mặc quần áo thể thao thoải mái và mang theo bình nước. Giày thể thao là bắt buộc — không được đi chân trần trong phòng gym.
Thành viên: Tất nhiên rồi. Buổi hướng dẫn mất bao lâu?
Huấn luyện viên: Khoảng một tiếng. Chúng ta sẽ tìm hiểu tất cả các máy và tôi sẽ giúp bạn đặt ra mục tiêu cá nhân.
Thành viên: Nghe thật hữu ích. Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều, David.
Huấn luyện viên: Rất vui được giúp. Hẹn gặp bạn vào thứ Năm, và chúc bạn có buổi bơi đầu tiên vui vẻ!

questions.prompt: When did Lisa join the sports club?
questions.explanation: Lisa says 'I just joined the club last week.'
questions.hint: She mentions the exact time in her first sentence.

questions.prompt: Which two activities does Lisa want to try?
questions.explanation: 'I'd like to try swimming and maybe some gym work.'
questions.hint: She mentions a water sport and exercise indoors.

questions.prompt: What time does the pool open?
questions.explanation: 'The pool is open from six in the morning until nine at night.'

questions.prompt: When does Lisa need to book a lane in advance?
questions.explanation: 'At weekends, it's better to book in advance because it gets quite busy.'
questions.hint: On weekdays you can just turn up.

questions.prompt: What must all new members do before using the gym?
questions.explanation: 'All new members must complete an induction before using the gym equipment.'
questions.hint: It is a requirement, not optional.

questions.prompt: When is Lisa's induction session booked for?
questions.explanation: 'I can book you in for one this Thursday at five o'clock.'
questions.hint: The day and time are both mentioned.

questions.prompt: What does the coach say Lisa must bring to the induction?
options: Sports clothes and a water bottle
questions.explanation: 'Just wear comfortable sports clothes and bring a water bottle.'

questions.prompt: What footwear rule does the coach mention?
options: Trainers are essential — no bare feet
questions.explanation: 'Trainers are essential — no bare feet in the gym.'
questions.hint: He gives a specific reason for the rule.

questions.prompt: How long does the induction session take?
questions.hint: The coach gives an approximate duration.

questions.prompt: What will the coach help Lisa do during the induction?
options: Sign up for a swimming class
questions.explanation: 'I'll help you set some personal goals.'
questions.hint: This is about Lisa's own fitness ambitions.

questions.prompt: Why does Lisa say she is nervous?
options: She is not a good swimmer
She does not know the coach
She has never trained at a sports club before
She is afraid of the gym equipment
questions.explanation: 'I've never really trained at a sports club before, so I'm a bit nervous.'

cloze.template:
Member: Hi, I just {{b1}} the club last week. I'm not sure where to start.
Coach: Welcome! My name's David. I'm the head {{b2}} here. What's your name?
Member: I'm Lisa. I've never really trained at a sports club before, so I'm a bit nervous.
Coach: Don't worry, everyone feels that way at the beginning. What sport are you {{b3}} in?
Member: I'd like to try swimming and maybe some gym work. I want to get {{b4}}.
Coach: That's a great combination. The pool is open from six in the morning until nine at {{b5}}.
Member: That's good. Do I need to book a lane, or can I just turn up?
Coach: For the pool, you can just turn up on weekdays. At weekends, it's better to book in {{b6}} because it gets quite busy.
Member: OK, I'll keep that in mind. What about the gym? Is there an {{b7}} session?
Coach: Yes, all new members must complete an induction before using the gym {{b8}}. I can book you in for one this Thursday at five o'clock.
Member: Thursday at five works perfectly for me. Do I need to bring anything?
Coach: Just wear comfortable sports clothes and bring a water {{b9}}. Trainers are essential — no bare feet in the gym.
Member: Of course. How long does the induction take?
Coach: About an {{b10}}. We'll go through all the machines and I'll help you set some personal goals.

blanks.explanation: 'joined' — you join a club when you become a member.
'coach' — he is introduced as the head coach.
'interested' — 'interested in' is the correct collocation for hobbies and topics.
'fitter' — the passage says 'I want to get fitter.'
'night' — the pool is open 'until nine at night.'
'advance' — 'book in advance' means to reserve something before you need it.
'induction' — an induction session is required for new gym members.
'equipment' — 'gym equipment' refers to all machines and tools in the gym.
'hour' — 'about an hour' gives the approximate duration.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Lisa says she wants to get fitter and has chosen swimming and gym work. What do you think are the best types of exercise for someone who is just starting out, and what advice would you give to a complete beginner at a sports club?
